summary: Justin Brennan coaches multifamily investors on market setup and broker relationship building. He explains how to use Crexy not to find deals (which he says are mostly overpriced at 80% of market), but to identify market-dominating brokers by their logo frequency. The core strategy involves calling brokers on listed deals as a pretext to build relationships and access their pre-market deal flow, where 80% of actual deals sell before public listing.
takeaways: Use Crexy to find brokers by logo frequency, not deals—brokers with 3+ listings are market makers with 3-4 more deals behind the scenes
Brokers filter off-market opportunities using loan maturity dates; properties with loans maturing in next 12 months are targets for refinance or sale conversations
Set up your market geographically (e.g., Ohio), then plan 2-3 day in-person trip to tour deals and meet brokers, PMs, and local lenders
Call brokers on Crexy-listed deals with script: 'I'm buying in this market, interested in this property, can I get on your preferred/pre-market list?'
Negotiations use price anchoring—start high (e.g., $100K concession), expect rejection, land on target range ($30-40K) after multiple counteroffers and cooling-off periods
Brokers give lists (not recommendations) of PM companies and local lenders to build your local team without broker endorsement
Stage 1 deals sell 80% of the time in broker's pre-market database of 25-50 investors; Stage 2 (Crexy) gets the rejects that need heavy negotiation

skill_candidates.change: systematically identify and prioritize brokers by market presence using Crexy logo frequency as signal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches filtering Crexy by broker logos in bottom-right corner, showing which brokers have most listings = market makers with hidden deal flow

skill_candidates.change: build pre-market access by calling on listed deals as relationship-building pretext, not actual deal targets
skill_candidates.evidence: 'I could care less about these properties... You're using them as a lure. You're not using them to actually do the deal.' Justin shows calling Austin Hall on 10-unit to gain access to his pre-market database

skill_candidates.change: recognize that 99% of Crexy deals are overpriced rejects from Stage 1; filter by loan maturity dates instead of property quality
skill_candidates.evidence: 'If it doesn't sell there because everybody passes on it, then it goes to stage two, which is what you're looking at here on krexy.com... it just needs to be heavily negotiated'

skill_candidates.change: use multi-stage price anchoring with 24-48 hour cooling periods to move sellers from hard 'no' toward realistic concession range
skill_candidates.evidence: Samir/Luis negotiation example: set $100K anchor, get 'no', come back with $50K, get rejection, land at $35-40K target after broker mediation and time delays

title: Weekly Coaching_10-30-25 | Justin Brennan
